title: Release agent-utils v1.0.2 for kitty image preview extension

title: Realtime plugin: expose unified /rt usage through controls API
description: Follow-up after the command vocabulary and controls API work: the canonical /rt usage text is local to the slash-command handler, so future UI/extensions using pi.realtime cannot display the same help text without duplicating it. Move the usage string to a shared constant, expose usage()/help() on pi.realtime, route /rt help through it, add tests, and document the API. Filed from user request to continue realtime beads.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Realtime plugin: fix status widget hide/off lifecycle
description: Audit finding from bd-db8405: /rt-hide-status clears the widget but does not set config.statusWidgetVisible=false, so later updateStatus calls can recreate the widget. Make hide/off/status transitions explicit and ensure /rt-off reliably clears realtime widget/status UI even if model restoration fails.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Implement Rust skill-server utility with MCP stdio meta-tool
description:
Implement a new Rust-based agent-utils utility called `skill-server` (`ss`) anchored around the `mcp-cli` crate, using Tendril as the reference for CLI/MCP stdio structure.

Goal:
- Provide both a CLI and stdio MCP server for dynamic skill/tool discovery without requiring agents to fully reload.
- Expose a single meta-tool / slash entrypoint for agents, conceptually `/ss <domain> <query-or-command>`.
- Example: `/ss web query ...` discovers a registered web-search MCP tool/server on the host and calls it.

Configuration:
- Read host configuration from `.config/ss/config.yaml` by default, with env/flag override if practical.
- Config should define skill search paths and MCP server definitions, e.g. paths to scan for Pi/Cacophony skills plus named MCP stdio servers/commands.
- Config should allow discovery of skills and other host MCP servers, including metadata needed to route a request to a matching server/tool.

Implementation expectations:
- Rust workspace/crate for `skill-server`/`ss`.
- Use or mirror Tendril's `mcp-cli` usage for JSON envelopes, CLI/MCP parity, and `mcp stdio` entrypoint design.
- Keep CLI and MCP execution paths unified.
- Implement a skeleton that builds first, with typed config loading, discovery abstractions, and a minimal meta-tool call path.
- Add tests/smokes for config parsing and MCP/CLI surface where feasible.

Acceptance criteria:
- `ss` (or `skill-server`) has a working CLI help surface.
- `ss mcp stdio` starts a stdio MCP server using mcp-cli-style plumbing.
- `.config/ss/config.yaml` sample/default is documented and supports search paths plus MCP server definitions.
- A minimal `/ss`-style meta request can discover a configured skill/server/tool and return a structured result or clear structured error.
- Build/test instructions are documented.

Filed from agnt-ctrl after Harry requested a new utility skill-server and pointed to Tendril's MCP CLI usage as the example.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Realtime plugin: route legacy aliases through unified /rt handler
description: Follow-up after bd-fd15b0/bd-5e0b27: the explicit /rt command vocabulary now exists, but several legacy slash commands still duplicate the same command logic inline. Route compatible aliases (/rt-on, /rt-off, /rt-audio, /rt-reasoning, /rt-listen, /rt-status, /rt-doctor, /rt-hide-status) through the unified /rt handler so future behavior changes stay centralized, preserving /rt-stop commit semantics where it differs. Add/adjust tests if needed. Filed from user request to continue realtime beads.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Realtime plugin: expose supported command modes in controls API
description: Follow-up after bd-78ed46: /rt start, mic, and stt modes are now validated, but pi.realtime.options() only exposes voice/backend/reasoning values. Expose supported start/mic/stt command modes through the control options API, update docs, and cover with tests so future UI affordances can discover every validated selector from one place. Filed from user request to continue realtime beads.

title: Anchor kitty image preview with Unicode placeholders in tmux

title: Add Firecracker VM integration to CACO control plane
description:
Integrate the Firecracker VM controller with CACO (Cacao) control plane by creating a wire protocol/endpoint that allows:
- CACO agents to discover and manage Firecracker VMs
- Communication between caco-ctrl and Tendril agents managing VMs
- Registration of VMs as available execution environments
- Proper orchestration and scheduling of workloads to Firecracker VMs

Acceptance criteria:
- caco-ctrl can communicate with Firecracker VMs via the integration point
- CACO agents can dispatch work to Firecracker VMs
- VM availability is visible to the CACO control plane
- Integration handles VM lifecycle events
creator: ms-mac:_:node-token

title: Realtime plugin: normalize /rt voice arguments
description: Follow-up after /rt tuning command hardening: realtime voice names are all lowercase, but /rt voice passes user input through verbatim so mixed-case values such as Verse are rejected even though the intended option is unambiguous. Normalize voice input to lowercase before validation, keep unsupported values guarded, and add tests/docs. Filed from user request to continue realtime beads.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Rename ss binary to skill-search to avoid iproute2 conflict
description: Home-manager buildEnv fails because agent-utils exports bin/ss, which conflicts with iproute2's bin/ss. Rename the agent-utils binary to skill-search and update references. Filed from operator quick-fix request.

title: Realtime plugin tests need a cleaner WebSocket injection seam

I worked around it by loading a data-URL copy with a fake WebSocket class. A cleaner fix would extract the WebSocket constructor behind an injectable factory or module-local test seam so realtime unit tests do not need source-string patching. title: Implement Firecracker VM controller for Tendril agents
description:
Create a Firecracker VM controller that can be orchestrated by Tendril agents, similar to how kitty images and Playwright are used. The VM should:
- Be controllable by Tendril agents
- Support running browsers and other configurable services
- Provide a visible screen interface comparable to existing kitty/Playwright implementations
- Include proper VM lifecycle management and initialization

Acceptance criteria:
- Firecracker VM can be spawned and controlled by Tendril agents
- Screen output is visible and accessible
- VM is configurable for different workloads (browsers, etc)
- Lifecycle events are properly tracked
creator: ms-mac:_:node-token

title: Seed GitHub Pages with tool inventory
description:
Create an initial GitHub Pages site for this repository that publishes a concise inventory of the tools and first-party surfaces available to agents/operators.

Scope:
- Add/enable a minimal GitHub Pages-compatible docs site for the repo.
- Include a tool inventory covering the Cacophony surfaces used by this project, such as bd, agent, msg, scratch, test, build, choices, cron, image generation, Pi loop/self-nudge/self-ops, tmux-cli, Tendril, and relevant repo-local tools.
- Keep the inventory generated or easy to update where practical; avoid duplicating stale long-form docs unnecessarily.
- Document how to preview/build the Pages content locally.
- Ensure the site can be published by GitHub Pages without requiring secrets or local-only state.

Acceptance criteria:

- The repo documents the Pages publishing path and local preview/build command.
- Validation appropriate for the chosen static site setup passes.

This can confuse quick binary rename validation; a short repo note or script should remind agents to stage renamed files before Nix flake builds, or run a preflight that detects untracked files referenced from Cargo.toml. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-4e57b1 session.

Add guarded /rt voice/backend/reasoning handling, tests, and docs. Filed from user request to continue realtime beads.

title: Add kitty graphics-backed Pi theme package
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-04-29: investigate/implement shipping a Pi theme as part of the agent-utils Pi plugin/package, backed by the existing kitty graphics library with a TypeScript implementation.

Context / feasibility:
- Pi packages can ship themes: package.json supports a `pi.themes` entry and conventional `themes/` directories; packages can also ship extensions alongside themes.
- Pi themes themselves are JSON color-token definitions, but a theme+extension combination can provide richer custom rendering using the extension APIs.
- caco-tui has good Rust kitty graphics examples, but the Rust crate is not currently FFI/linkable/callable from Pi TypeScript, so implement a TypeScript renderer for now rather than trying to link the Rust crate.

Requested direction:
- Build a Pi package/extension/theme combination that uses agent-utils' kitty graphics protocol/library to render freeform graphical UI affordances.
- Prototype a small TypeScript renderer that can render lines/borders/styles into actual byte/image form suitable for kitty graphics display and Unicode placeholder placement.
- Use kitty Unicode placeholder characters to display graphical output at theme/UI locations, e.g. replacing simple ASCII prompt enclosure lines like `-----` with rendered graphics.
- Explore graphical/translucent/gradient borders around tables, agent/code blocks, or other message blocks.
- Keep the implementation in TypeScript for Pi; consult caco-tui Rust examples only as a reference for protocol/rendering behavior.
- Package any theme assets through `themes/` or package.json `pi.themes`, and any runtime behavior through the Pi extension package entries.
- Add docs/examples explaining how to install/select the theme and how the extension cooperates with the theme.
- Add tests for renderer byte output/protocol generation and for package manifest discovery where practical.

Important constraints:
- Do not globally clear/delete kitty graphics images; coordinate with bd-f89780's scoped image ownership/cleanup work.
- Avoid redrawing/destroying the Caco/Pi UI; all image placement/deletion should be scoped to the extension-owned images/placeholders.
- Preserve existing terminal/tmux placeholder behavior.

Controller triage notes:
- Pi docs confirm packages can ship themes via package.json `pi.themes` or conventional `themes/` directories.
- Duplicate searches for `kitty theme`, `unicode placeholder theme`, and `graphical borders` found no matching bead across all statuses.
- Main-history keyword check found prior kitty graphics/image preview work, but not this theme-backed graphical rendering feature.
- Depends on bd-f89780 because scoped image ownership/cleanup is prerequisite safety for using kitty graphics to decorate core UI regions.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Realtime plugin: align defaults documentation with Pulse-first setup
description: Audit finding from bd-db8405 plus operator correction: the source header describes PI_RT_AUDIO_BACKEND auto behaviour, while the extension intentionally defaults to Pulse because the normal setup often uses Pulse even on macOS with phone sink/source. Update docs/comments/status text so this intended Pulse-first default is clear without suggesting the code should switch away from Pulse.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Realtime plugin: validate /rt status arguments
description: Follow-up after realtime command validation work: /rt status currently treats any argument other than full as compact status, so typos such as /rt status ful silently produce compact output. Validate status modes, warn without changing widget state for unsupported values, expose statusModes through pi.realtime.options(), and add tests/docs. Filed from user request to continue realtime beads.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Profile audit: controller reified instructions include worker lifecycle conflicts
description: Profile audit finding from pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl reified instructions. The actual reified instruction files above checkout combine generic worker lifecycle constraints with the project-controller persistent role. Generic Managed Agent Constraints say every managed agent is working an assigned bead, should start by running caco bd show, and when finished must commit and run caco agent complete. The project-controller profile for this same agent says it is a persistent controller, must not pick up worker tasks or implement application code, must not call caco agent complete, and should use reintegrate only for controller-owned operational changes. There is also recurring-task ambiguity: generic guidance says to use daemon-managed caco cron for recurring tasks, while project-controller/Pi guidance says per-controller self-check loops should stay inside the Pi runtime and not rely on daemon-global cron. Impact: persistent controllers receive contradictory lifecycle/startup/scheduling instructions and may mistakenly behave as bead workers. Please triage/profile-materialization so persistent controllers get role-specific lifecycle guidance without worker-only completion/hydration requirements and clarify scheduling precedence.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Realtime plugin: validate /rt audio and widget arguments
description: Follow-up after bd-78ed46: /rt start, mic, and stt modes are validated, but /rt audio <arg> and /rt widget <arg> still accept arbitrary values and fall through to toggle/show behavior. Validate audio/widget arguments, warn without changing state for unsupported values, and add tests/docs. Filed from user request to continue realtime beads.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Audit and polish realtime plugin Pi API UX controls
description: Operator request: pull latest and audit extensions/realtime-agent.js quality; improve UX control within the Pi API for realtime conversations, which currently works but feels rough/flaky. Filed by l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9 from interactive instruction.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Realtime plugin: include diagnostic error fields in control snapshots
description: Follow-up after the realtime control API polish: /rt-doctor exposes last response/playback errors and mic counters, but pi.realtime.snapshot() only exposes high-level state. Add a small diagnostics/health block to snapshots with last response/playback errors and mic counters so UI consumers can surface problems without parsing /rt-doctor text. Add tests/docs. title: Realtime plugin: add rt-doctor diagnostics
description: Audit finding from bd-db8405: audio/API failures are hard to diagnose. Add /rt-doctor or /rt-status full showing resolved provider/backend, record/playback commands, Pulse/CoreAudio/device hints, API/model config, last mic/playback errors, connection phase, and actionable remediation notes. Pulse should remain the normal default, including macOS phone sink/source workflows.

title: Improve kitty graphics multiviewer navigation and scoped cleanup
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-04-29: improve the Pi kitty graphics image display extension/multiviewer.

Requested behavior:
- Add controls/commands for the multiviewer to move through images explicitly: e.g. /kitty-show-next and /kitty-show-prev.
- Add a timed cycling mode: e.g. /kitty-start-cycle 5 to cycle images every 5 seconds and /kitty-stop-cycle to stop. If there is an existing command naming convention, follow it, but provide equivalent next/previous/start/stop-cycle behavior.
- Make the multiviewer UI expose these controls clearly enough for users.
- Fix image cleanup/deletion: the plugin appears to be killing all kitty graphics images in the entire terminal. When run inside caco this redraws/destroys the entire UI. The extension must only manage/delete the kitty images it created.

Implementation guidance:
- Track image IDs / placements / namespaces owned by this extension, and scope clear/delete operations to those owned resources only.
- Avoid global kitty graphics clear/delete sequences that affect unrelated images or the caco UI.
- Preserve existing tmux/placeholder placement behavior and current preview commands.
- Add/update tests and docs for navigation, cycle controls, and scoped cleanup behavior.
- Verify with the project’s normal test suite or targeted tests if the full suite is not practical.

Controller triage notes:
- Existing closed bead bd-74597a covers the original Pi kitty graphics extension, not these multiviewer/cycle/scoped-cleanup changes.
- Search/main-history check did not find an already-landed multiviewer cycle/scoped cleanup change on main before filing this bead.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Realtime plugin: simplify slash command semantics
description: Audit finding from bd-db8405: /rt, /rt ptt, /rt nolisten, /rt stt, /rt-on, /rt-off, /rt-stop, and /rt-listen have overloaded meanings that are hard to predict. Redesign commands around explicit actions such as start/stop, mic vad|ptt|off, audio on|off, stt start|stop, widget show|hide, while preserving compatibility aliases where practical.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9
